(Redirected from [Snapdragon S4](/w/index.php?title=Snapdragon_S4&redirect=no "Snapdragon S4"))

The [Snapdragon](/wiki/Qualcomm_Snapdragon "Qualcomm Snapdragon") is a brand of [systems on chips](/wiki/System_on_a_chip "System on a chip") (SoCs) designed by [Qualcomm](/wiki/Qualcomm "Qualcomm") and intended for [mobile computing](/wiki/Mobile_computing "Mobile computing") use.

## Acronyms

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_systems_on_chips&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Acronyms")]

System on chips released before the *Snapdragon* naming scheme are referred to only by their [stock keeping unit](/wiki/Stock_keeping_unit "Stock keeping unit") (SKU) numbers. Some models were later given a Snapdragon product name. Earlier models are prefixed by an [acronymic](/wiki/Acronym "Acronym") three-letter code; their full forms are as follows:[[1]](#cite_note-1)

* QSC *(Qualcomm Single Chip)*
* MSM *(Mobile Station Modem)*, denotes a chip with integrated [cellular modem](/wiki/Mobile_broadband_modem "Mobile broadband modem")
* APQ *(Application Processor Qualcomm)*, denotes a chip without modem[[2]](#cite_note-2)

### Snapdragon S4

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_systems_on_chips&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Snapdragon S4")]

**Snapdragon S4** was offered in three models: **S4 Play** for budget and entry-level devices, **S4 Plus** for mid-range devices and **S4 Pro** for high-end devices.[[32]](#cite_note-Snapdragon_S4-32) It was launched in 2012. The Snapdragon S4 were succeeded by the Snapdragon 200/400 series (S4 Play) and 600/800 series (S4 Plus and S4 Pro).

## Snapdragon 6 series

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_systems_on_chips&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Snapdragon 6 series")]

Further information: [Devices using Qualcomm Snapdragon processors § Snapdragon 6 series](/wiki/Devices_using_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_processors#Snapdragon_6_series "Devices using Qualcomm Snapdragon processors")

The Snapdragon 6 series is the mid-range SoC primarily targeted at both the entry-level and mid-range segments, succeeding the S4 Plus. It is the most commonly used Snapdragon line-up, appearing in mainstream devices of various manufacturers.

### Snapdragon 600 series (2013–2023)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_systems_on_chips&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Snapdragon 600 series (2013–2023)")]

Unlike the later models of the 600 series, Snapdragon 600 was considered a high-end SoC similar to the Snapdragon 800, and was the direct successor of both the Snapdragon S4 Plus and S4 Pro. The Snapdragon 615 was Qualcomm's first octa-core SoC. Starting with the Snapdragon 610, the 600 series became a mid-range SoC lineup, as opposed to the original Snapdragon 600, which was a high-end model.

### Snapdragon 6s 4G (since 2024)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_systems_on_chips&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Snapdragon 6s 4G (since 2024)")]

The 600 series concluded in 2023 with the 695. The 6s 4G series (2024–) targets cost-sensitive 4G-only markets using recycled architectures on mature nodes. Notable examples include the 6s 4G Gen 1 (rebadged SM6115 (Snapdragon
662) from 2020) and Gen 2 (rebadged SM6225 (Snapdragon
680/685) from 2021).

### Snapdragon 6 (since 2022)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_systems_on_chips&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Snapdragon 6 (since 2022)")]

Qualcomm adopted generational naming in 2022 with the Snapdragon 6 series. All models feature integrated 5G modems and Wi-Fi 6/6E. The "6s" variants offer reduced specifications at lower price points. The series transitioned from Samsung 4LPX (Gen 1) to TSMC N4P (Gen 4), achieving significant efficiency gains. The Gen 4 introduces Cortex-A720/A520 cores, marking the first departure from A78/A55 combinations in the lineup and bringing architecture parity with the 7- and 8-series designs albeit with smaller cores at lower clock speeds.

## Snapdragon 7 series

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_systems_on_chips&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Snapdragon 7 series")]

Further information: [Devices using Qualcomm Snapdragon processors § Snapdragon 7 series](/wiki/Devices_using_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_processors#Snapdragon_7_series "Devices using Qualcomm Snapdragon processors")

On February 27, 2018, Qualcomm Introduced the Snapdragon 7 Mobile Platform Series. It is an upper mid-range SoC designed to bridge the gap between the 6 series and the 8 series (the predecessor of Snapdragon 7 series is Snapdragon 660), and primarily aimed at premium mid-range segment.[[135]](#cite_note-137)

## Snapdragon 8 series

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_systems_on_chips&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Snapdragon 8 series")]

Further information: [Devices using Qualcomm Snapdragon processors § Snapdragon 8 series](/wiki/Devices_using_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_processors#Snapdragon_8_series "Devices using Qualcomm Snapdragon processors")

The Snapdragon 8 series is the high-end SoC and serves as Qualcomm's current flagship, succeeding the S4 Pro and the older S1/S2/S3 series.

## Snapdragon X series

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_systems_on_chips&action=edit&section=31 "Edit section: Snapdragon X series")]

The Snapdragon X series is a family of Qualcomm systems on chips (SoCs) designed primarily for Windows PCs and related form factors, using Arm-based CPU designs and integrating GPU and on-device AI acceleration in a single platform. The series was introduced with the Snapdragon X Elite in October 2023 and expanded with additional tiers including Snapdragon X Plus in April 2024 and Snapdragon X in January 2025, with the lineup positioned to span premium through more mainstream PC designs.[[288]](#cite_note-292)[[289]](#cite_note-293)[[290]](#cite_note-294)

### Snapdragon X1 series

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_systems_on_chips&action=edit&section=32 "Edit section: Snapdragon X1 series")]

The Snapdragon X1 series comprises the first generation of Snapdragon X laptop-focused SoCs, including Snapdragon X Elite (model prefix X1E), Snapdragon X Plus (X1P), and Snapdragon X (X1), which share common platform elements such as Qualcomm’s Oryon CPU branding, Adreno integrated graphics, and a Hexagon NPU specified by Qualcomm at up to 45 TOPS for on-device AI workloads across multiple X1 configurations.

### Snapdragon X2 series

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_systems_on_chips&action=edit&section=33 "Edit section: Snapdragon X2 series")]

The Snapdragon X2 series is the successor generation to the X1 series for Windows PCs, introduced by Qualcomm in late 2025 with higher-tier Snapdragon X2 Elite and X2 Elite Extreme variants and followed in early 2026 by the Snapdragon X2 Plus tier.[[298]](#cite_note-:3-302) Qualcomm’s published materials describe the X2 generation as increasing the platform’s on-device AI capability to an 80 TOPS Hexagon NPU and scaling CPU configurations up to 18 Oryon-branded cores in the flagship tier, while product announcements and coverage indicate initial device availability in 2026.

## Mixed Reality (MR) platforms

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_systems_on_chips&action=edit&section=40 "Edit section: Mixed Reality (MR) platforms")]

### Snapdragon XR series

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_systems_on_chips&action=edit&section=41 "Edit section: Snapdragon XR series")]

* **Snapdragon XR1** was announced in May 2018, is designed for [Augmented reality](/wiki/Augmented_reality "Augmented reality"), [Virtual reality](/wiki/Virtual_reality "Virtual reality") and [mixed reality](/wiki/Mixed_reality "Mixed reality").[[365]](#cite_note-370)
* **Snapdragon XR2** was announced in December 2019, and is a derivative of the Snapdragon 865.[[366]](#cite_note-371)[[367]](#cite_note-Qualcomm_Snapdragon_XR2_announcement-372)

:   It is used in the [Meta Quest 2](/wiki/Meta_Quest_2 "Meta Quest 2"), the [HTC Vive Focus 3](/wiki/HTC_Vive#Product_lines "HTC Vive") and the [Pico 4](/wiki/Pico_4 "Pico 4").

* **Snapdragon XR2+ Gen 1** was announced on October 11, 2022,[[368]](#cite_note-373) and is used in the [Meta Quest Pro](/wiki/Meta_Quest_Pro "Meta Quest Pro").
* **Snapdragon XR2 Gen 2** is announced in September 2023.[[369]](#cite_note-MR2023-374) Qualcomm claims improved performance compared to its predecessor XR2 5G.

:   The SoC can handle up to 10 concurrent sensors & cameras, per-eye resolution of 3K x 3K and 12ms full-color video pass-through.[[370]](#cite_note-375)
:   It supports [Wi-Fi 7](/wiki/Wi-Fi_7 "Wi-Fi 7") network. It is integrated into [Meta Quest 3](/wiki/Meta_Quest_3 "Meta Quest 3") and [Pico 4 Ultra](/wiki/PICO_4 "PICO 4").

* **Snapdragon XR2+ Gen 2** is an [overclocked](/wiki/Overclocking "Overclocking") version of the XR2 Gen 2.[[371]](#cite_note-376) It is integrated into [Samsung Galaxy XR](/wiki/Samsung_Galaxy_XR "Samsung Galaxy XR").

### Snapdragon AR series

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_systems_on_chips&action=edit&section=42 "Edit section: Snapdragon AR series")]

The Qualcomm **Snapdragon AR2 Gen 1 Platform** was announced November 17, 2022.[[378]](#cite_note-383) It is intended for use in smart glasses.[[379]](#cite_note-384)
  
On September 27, 2023 Qualcomm announced the **Snapdragon AR1 Gen 1 Platform** for slim and light [AR](/wiki/Augmented_reality "Augmented reality") glasses.[[369]](#cite_note-MR2023-374) It is designed to enable personal assistants, audio quality enhancement, visual search, and real-time translation using on-device AI acceleration. The platform supports binocular displays with up to 1280 x 1280 resolution for heads-up information and also content consumption. The new 14-bit ISP can capture 12MP photos and 6MP video recording & live-streaming. Head tracking is limited to 3DoF (three degrees of freedom).

## Gaming platforms

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_systems_on_chips&action=edit&section=43 "Edit section: Gaming platforms")]

### Snapdragon G series

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_systems_on_chips&action=edit&section=44 "Edit section: Snapdragon G series")]

In December 2021, Qualcomm announced the Snapdragon G3x Gen 1 Gaming Platform.[[380]](#cite_note-Snapdragon_G3x_Gen_1-385)[[381]](#cite_note-Qualcomm_Snapdragon_G3x_Gen_1_announcement-386) The [Razer Edge](/wiki/Razer_Edge "Razer Edge") is the first device to use the platform.[[382]](#cite_note-387)

The G3x Gen 1 is based on the Snapdragon 888+ as it has the same motherboard code name ([Lahaina](/wiki/Lahaina "Lahaina")), the same CPU clusters with higher clock speeds, and the same GPU with a higher clock speed. Connectivity options also seem in line.[[383]](#cite_note-388)

In August 2023, Qualcomm announced the Snapdragon G series platform designed for handheld gaming devices.[[384]](#cite_note-389)

On March 19, 2025, Qualcomm revealed the Snapdragon G3 Gen 3, G2 Gen 2, and G1 Gen 2 platforms.[[385]](#cite_note-390)

## Bluetooth SoC platforms

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Qualcomm_Snapdragon_systems_on_chips&action=edit&section=45 "Edit section: Bluetooth SoC platforms")]

Following Qualcomm's acquisition of [CSR](/wiki/CSR_plc "CSR plc") in 2015, Qualcomm designs ultra-low-power [Bluetooth](/wiki/Bluetooth "Bluetooth") [SoCs](/wiki/System_on_a_chip "System on a chip") under the CSR, QCA and QCC brands for wireless headphones and earbuds. Qualcomm has worked with both [Amazon](/wiki/Amazon_(company) "Amazon (company)") and [Google](/wiki/Google "Google") on reference designs to help manufacturers develop headsets with support for [Alexa](/wiki/Amazon_Alexa "Amazon Alexa"), [Google Assistant](/wiki/Google_Assistant "Google Assistant") and [Google Fast Pair](/wiki/Google_Fast_Pair "Google Fast Pair").[[394]](#cite_note-399)[[395]](#cite_note-400) Qualcomm announced the QCC5100 Series at CES 2018.[[396]](#cite_note-401)

On January 28, 2020, the QCC304x and QCC514x SoCs were published as Bluetooth 5.2 certified by the Bluetooth SIG.[[397]](#cite_note-:10-402)[[398]](#cite_note-:11-403) On the previous day Qualcomm published a blog post on LE Audio, referring to the QCC5100 series.[[399]](#cite_note-404) On March 25, 2020, the [BLE Audio](/wiki/Bluetooth_Low_Energy#Audio "Bluetooth Low Energy") QCC304x and QCC514x SoCs were officially announced.[[400]](#cite_note-405)[[401]](#cite_note-406)
